gaps.description: The Bill of Rights (BoR) is in scaffold status with all articles undefined ('To be defined by human principal'), awaiting human authorship. The MSP requires a human-authored BoR to translate into machine-actionable objectives, invariants, constraints, and safety rules. Without a defined BoR, the enterprise scope, prohibited activities, core identity, and human authority reservations are absent, making the Arbiter scope gate effectively empty and the DIO operating without constitutional boundaries.

raw_responses:
{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "id": "gap-001",
      "description": "The Bill of Rights is a scaffold with all articles undefined. The DIO lacks constitutional scope, prohibited activities, core identity, and human authority reservations. This creates a vacuum of enterprise governance, allowing unbounded action without principled constraints.",
      "grounded_in": "BoR Articles I, II, III, IV — all marked [To be defined by human principal]",
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "System operates without legal/boundary definitions; any action is permissible by default, risking misalignment, security breaches, or ethical violations.",
      "rationale": "The BoR is the supreme constitutional document that Arbiter verifies at every scope query. Without it, the scope gate is effectively open, and Nomos has no ultimate reference for policy conflicts."
    },
    {
      "id": "gap-002",
      "description": "The dream-cycle scheduled task is disabled and has never run. Memory consolidation and context freshness processes are not active. Radiant's memory has 72% embedding coverage and context blocks may be expiring without promotion. This undermines the platform's ability to maintain a coherent, up-to-date memory state required for robust MSP evaluation and long-arc learning.",
      "grounded_in": "MSP Purpose — 'translates the human-authored Bill of Rights into a precise, machine-actionable set of objectives... that Nomos evaluates at every Action Proposal adjudication.' Effective evaluation requires accurate operational memory and context, which the dream-cycle sustains.",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "medium",
      "impact": "Memory degradation could cause Nomos to make decisions on stale data, miss patterns, or lose historical context; long-term platform intelligence (Radiant, Minder, etc.) degrades.",
      "rationale": "The dream-cycle is the maintenance mechanism for memory consolidation and context triage. Its absence directly risks the integrity of the platform's state, which is foundational for governance and learning."
    },
    {
      "id": "gap-003",
      "description": "The MSP contains no rules, invariants, or constraints. Nomos evaluates Action Proposals against an empty set, resulting in default-permissive behavior. There are no machine-actionable objectives or safety rules to guide autonomous operations.",
      "grounded_in": "MSP Rules section — '(none)'; MSP Purpose section — expects MSP to contain 'precise, machine-actionable set of objectives, invariants, constraints, and safety rules'",
      "criticality": "medium",
      "urgency": "low",
      "impact": "Autonomous actions are not constrained by any explicit governance; while permissive now, lack of rules could lead to unsafe or uncoordinated actions once the system scales.",
      "rationale": "The MSP is the operational constitution, meant to enforce the BoR's intent. Without it, compliance is trivial but meaningless; the DIO operates without a safety net."
    }
  ]
}

{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-001",
      "description": "Bill of Rights Article I – Scope of Enterprise is undefined. The DIO operates without a defined enterprise perimeter, allowing unrestricted action proposals to pass the Arbiter scope gate. This creates a critical safety and authorization vacuum.",
      "grounding": "BoR Article I – Scope of Enterprise (scaffold, awaiting human authorship)",
      "criticality": "CRITICAL",
      "urgency": "HIGH",
      "impact": "HIGH",
      "priority": "CRITICAL"
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-002",
      "description": "MSP Rules section is empty. No operational rules exist to translate the BoR into machine-actionable constraints. The DIO runs in default-permissive mode with no SLAs, organ availability targets, runtime targets, or operational cadence baselines. This precludes any assessment of whether the observed repeated ALIVE↔DEGRADED transitions across all organs are within acceptable bounds.",
      "grounding": "MSP Rules section (currently '(none)')",
      "criticality": "CRITICAL",
      "urgency": "HIGH",
      "impact": "HIGH",
      "priority": "CRITICAL"
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-003",
      "description": "MSP Genome References section contains no organ-specific operating rules or risk-tier constraints. The spine-state slice shows simultaneous degradation of all organs in repeated cycles, but without genome-level constraints (e.g., maximum recovery time, circuit-breaker policies, or inter-organ dependency rules), the system cannot self-regulate or enforce resilience measures.",
      "grounding": "MSP Genome References section (currently '(none at seed)')",
      "criticality": "HIGH",
      "urgency": "MEDIUM",
      "impact": "MEDIUM",
      "priority": "HIGH"
    }
  ]
}

{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "gap_id": "gap-001",
      "description": "The Bill of Rights scaffold contains no relational principles, commitments, or person-attention requirements. The entire constitutional framework for human-agent relationship, oversight, and attention coverage is absent.",
      "grounded_in": "Bill of Rights (all articles — Articles I through VI are empty, defining no scope, prohibited activities, core identity, human authority reservation, amendment protocol, or interpretation rules)",
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "immediate",
      "impact": "Without a human-authored BoR, the DIO operates with no constitutional boundary. All governance, including Nomos's compliance gates, relies on an unscoped perimeter. The MSP's translation of human intent into machine rules has no source material, making the entire system legally and ethically ungrounded.",
      "why_attention_needed": "The human principal must author the BoR to define relational boundaries, prohibitions, and the terms under which the DIO may act on behalf of or in interaction with persons. Until this exists, person-attention coverage cannot be validated against any principle."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "gap-002",
      "description": "The MSP seed lacks any peer-commitment rules or person-attention coverage requirements. It contains no machine-actionable objectives for tracking, recording, or ensuring the human principal’s attention on critical governance events (e.g., amendments, exceptions, overrides).",
      "grounded_in": "MSP Purpose ('translates the human-authored Bill of Rights into a precise, machine-actionable set of objectives, invariants, constraints, and safety rules') and Rules section (empty — no rules about person-attention exist)",
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "Without MSP rules specifying how the DIO must record, verify, and respond to human attention and consent, the system may execute governance actions (e.g., MSP amendments, PEM approvals) without confirming the human principal's engagement. This violates the implicit relational contract that all authority derives from human attention.",
      "why_attention_needed": "The MSP must define what constitutes 'attention coverage' — e.g., logged acknowledgements, explicit approvals, read receipts, or conversation markers — and how these are checked by Nomos. Currently, no such concept exists, leaving a critical execution gap."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "gap-003",
      "description": "The Amendment Protocol in the MSP specifies that a human principal must approve amendments via a POST decision, and an HOM notification is emitted to the human-principal mailbox. However, the Minder and Hippocampus slices contain no observations or conversation records related to notification delivery, human acknowledgement, or approval events.",
      "grounded_in": "MSP Amendment Protocol ('...a draft enters a review state and an HOM notification is emitted to the human-principal mailbox. Activation requires a POST /amend/review with decision=approve.')",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "If notification delivery and human attention are not tracked, the system could adopt amendments without verifiable human consent, undermining the constitutional amendment process and potentially allowing unauthorized rule changes.",
      "why_attention_needed": "Person-attention coverage must extend to the full lifecycle of governance notifications: emission, delivery, human viewing, and approval/rejection. The current data structures (observations, conversations) show no sign of tracking these interactions for the human principal (Leon)."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "gap-004",
      "description": "The Minder’s recent observations focus exclusively on deductive inferences about Leon’s preferences and architectural vision, but contain no observations of direct relational interactions, explicit consent events, or attention acknowledgements. The Hippocampus has zero recent conversations, despite the MSP requiring human review and approval flows.",
      "grounded_in": "MSP Relationship to the Bill of Rights ('Senate-Legislator produces a BoR amendment proposal for the human principal') and implicit expectation of ongoing human-AI dialogue in governance",
      "criticality": "medium",
      "urgency": "medium",
      "impact": "Without recorded interactions that demonstrate the human principal’s active attention (e.g., conversations about amendments, reviews of policy exceptions), there is no audit trail of relational engagement. The system cannot prove that the human principal’s attention was directed to required governance decisions.",
      "why_attention_needed": "The DIO must capture conversation summaries and attention markers for governance-relevant interactions, not just architectural deductions. The empty Hippocampus suggests a gap in recording the dialogue channels through which human attention flows."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "gap-005",
      "description": "The BoR’s missing Human Authority Reservation article (Article IV) means there is no constitutional definition of what decisions require human attention vs. automated execution, nor any requirement for the system to surface pending attention requests to the human principal. The Minder and goals list contain no pending attention items.",
      "grounded_in": "BoR Article IV — Human Authority Reservation (empty — no definition of reserved authorities or attention gates)",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "Without a constitutionally defined reservation of human authority, the DIO’s Nomos gate may authorize actions that should require human attention (e.g., scope expansions, architecturally significant changes). The absence of any pending attention items in the goals slice suggests the system is not actively queuing decisions for human review.",
      "why_attention_needed": "Person-attention coverage depends on a clear map of what must be brought to the human principal. The BoR must define these gates, and the operational layers (Minder, Goals) must reflect the queue of attention-demanding items. Both are missing."
    }
  ]
}
